goal: Diagnose a tutorial import_robot error -- stale API vs missing URDF asset -- without suggesting a reinstall.
thoughts: Read the actual error first via explain_error. 'Tutorial from 2 years ago' is a strong hint the API path changed. If URDF is genuinely missing, say so plainly. Never send him to reinstall -- that is the beginner-thrash trap. Have load_scene_template as the unblock-now fallback.
code:
translation = explain_error(
    error_text="<PASTED_CONSOLE_ERROR>",
    audience="beginner",
    include_fix=True
)
kb = lookup_knowledge(
    query="Isaac Sim 5.x URDF import current API entry point"
)
try:
    robot = import_robot(
        source_path="<HOME>/Downloads/some_robot.urdf",
        target_path="/World/Robot",
        fix_base=True,
        merge_fixed_joints=True
    )
except FileNotFoundError:
    # stale tutorial path or mesh missing
    pass
# Or load a USD robot directly:
create_prim(path="/World/Robot", type_name="Xform")
add_reference(prim_path="/World/Robot", reference_path="<FRANKA_USD>")
# Unblock fallback:
# load_scene_template(template_name="franka_pick_place")
failure_modes: Telling him to reinstall Isaac Sim
Pasting a new snippet without reading the actual error first
Missing that the tutorial uses a deprecated 4.x API path
